audit_result.proof_trace: Challenge computation at line 877 passes commitments_bytes (original array with duplicates) instead of unique_commitments (deduplicated array) to compute_verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch_challenge. This causes the challenge hash to include the first num_commitments elements from the original array, which may contain duplicates at different positions than the deduplicated set. Different orderings of semantically equivalent commitments produce different challenge values, violating cryptographic soundness.
audit_result.attack_scenario: Attacker crafts two P2P messages with identical unique commitments but different orderings (e.g., [C_A, C_A, C_B] vs [C_B, C_A, C_A]). The challenge function hashes different commitment sequences, generating different challenge values r1 ≠ r2 for semantically equivalent verification contexts, potentially accepting invalid proofs.

PROP-57888860-inv-003.text: Commitment deduplication must preserve semantic equivalence — mapping indices must be bijective per unique commitment.
PROP-57888860-inv-003.assertion: forall dedup(comms_bytes): forall i,j: comms_bytes[i] == comms_bytes[j] <=> commitment_indices[i] == commitment_indices[j]
